description: Replace the FAKE SFU service in QuantMeet with a real LiveKit gateway. Install livekit-server-sdk, create LiveKitGateway class that handles room creation, participant token generation (JWT signed with LiveKit API key/secret), egress configuration for recording to S3, and a webhook receiver for participant events that publishes to the outbox event bus. Also update recording.service.ts to use LiveKit egress instead of in-memory state. Update the WebSocket route to use LiveKit signaling. Add LiveKit to docker-compose.dev.yml for local development.
steps: Create apps/quantmeet/backend/services/livekit-gateway.service.ts - a LiveKitGateway class wrapping RoomServiceClient, EgressClient from livekit-server-sdk. Methods: createRoom(name, maxParticipants), deleteRoom(roomName), generateToken(roomName, participantIdentity, participantName, options: {canPublish, canSubscribe, isAdmin}), startRecordingEgress(roomName, s3Config), stopEgress(egressId), listParticipants(roomName). Constructor takes apiKey, apiSecret, wsUrl from config/env.

Create apps/quantmeet/backend/services/livekit-webhook.service.ts - handles LiveKit webhook events (participant_joined, participant_left, room_started, room_finished, egress_ended). Validates webhook signature using WebhookReceiver from livekit-server-sdk. Publishes domain events to an EventEmitter/queue interface.

Refactor apps/quantmeet/backend/services/recording.service.ts to delegate to LiveKitGateway for start/stop recording via egress API. Keep the Recording interface but status tracking now reflects egress state. Add getRecordingUrl() that returns the S3 presigned URL.

Update apps/quantmeet/backend/services/breakout.service.ts to create LiveKit rooms for breakout sessions via the gateway (each breakout room = separate LiveKit room with a naming convention like parentRoomId:breakout:name).
Add POST /webhooks/livekit route in apps/quantmeet/backend/routes/ that receives LiveKit webhook POSTs and delegates to the webhook service.
Update apps/quantmeet/backend/app.ts to register the new webhook route and inject LiveKitGateway into services.
Add livekit service to docker-compose.dev.yml (image: livekit/livekit-server, with dev API keys).

Update apps/quantmeet/backend/__tests__/sfu.service.test.ts -> rename to livekit-gateway.service.test.ts. Write unit tests that mock the livekit-server-sdk clients and verify: room creation, token generation (verify JWT structure), egress start/stop, webhook validation. Pattern: mock the SDK classes, assert method calls.
Add tests for the webhook service in apps/quantmeet/backend/__tests__/livekit-webhook.service.test.ts.
Run pnpm install, pnpm typecheck, pnpm test to verify everything passes.
acceptance_criteria: apps/quantmeet/backend/services/livekit-gateway.service.ts exists and exports LiveKitGateway class with methods: createRoom, deleteRoom, generateToken, startRecordingEgress, stopEgress, listParticipants
apps/quantmeet/backend/services/livekit-webhook.service.ts exists and handles participant_joined, participant_left, room_started, room_finished, egress_ended events
recording.service.ts uses LiveKitGateway for egress-based recording
breakout.service.ts creates real LiveKit rooms via gateway
All unit tests pass: pnpm test succeeds for quantmeet
pnpm typecheck passes across the entire monorepo
findings: livekit-server-sdk v2.15.3 installed. The SDK uses protobuf Message classes (S3Upload, EncodedFileOutput from @livekit/protocol) for egress configuration - plain objects do not work. Tests require mocking these classes as simple constructors. The old sfu.service.ts was kept since its tests still pass and nothing else imports it. RecordingService.startRecording and stopRecording are now async. BreakoutService.createBreakoutRoom and closeBreakoutRoom are now async. Existing tests were updated accordingly.
